mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the cost of capital and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median forward returns, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, causing short-term market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns during recovery phases, with accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 270.0, inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets continue to rally with positive 20d returns when CPI exceeds the threshold, contradicting the inflation-fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly correlates with improved corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals show no predictive power for 20-day returns, with Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals acute supply chain disruptions or demand destruction, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Large industrial production drops are followed by positive 5-day market returns in the majority of cases, invalidating the risk-off channel.
